questionsList.content: What is a business plan?

answersList.content: An Excel spreadsheet showing revenues and costs
A Power Point presentation for potential investors
A roadmap plan comprising a business design: how an idea can become a profitable sustained and growing business, and how the idea will be delivered

questionsList.content: What are the 5 key elements of every business plan?

answersList.content: The team; the technology; the profit margin; patents; competition
The need; the product; the unique features; future developments; the market
Interviewing potential clients, asking what they would pay

questionsList.content: What is an entrepreneur?

answersList.content: a salaried manager in a large corporation
a financial manager in a small firm
an owner-manager who buys out the founder of a firm

questionsList.content: Clara is an employee but she would like to have more control over her life and directly reap the rewards for her hard work. She would like to start her own business. What would likely have the strongest influence on Clara becoming an entrepreneur?

answersList.content: a satisfying lifestyle, independence, love of country
a satisfying lifestyle, financial rewards, preserving the capitalistic system
a satisfying lifestyle, independence, love of country

questionsList.content: Mark has started a cyber-security business to take advantage of new laws requiring all medical organizations to secure their data. This new industry is forecasted to generate several billion in sales per year. What type of business does Mark own?
Keith runs his landscaping company from April to October then closes it so he can travel the world to enjoy his love of skiing. Which entrepreneurial reward does Keith value most?
Only three numbers (value, price, cost) can help determine whether your idea is viable? Which of the three are most crucial?
John is finding it difficult to service his customers properly due to the wide variety tasks required to run his landscaping company. Which challenge is likely most difficult for John?

answersList.content: making decisions about how to invest excess cash balances

questionsList.content: "Social margin" is:

answersList.content: The gap between cost and price
The gap between value and price
The gap between cost and value

questionsList.content: Client margin is:

answersList.content: The gap between value and price
The gap between value and cost
The gap between cost and price

questionsList.content: Organizational structure and strategy are linked because strategy shows what to do, structure shows who does it
Supplier power, barriers to entry, rivalry, buyer power, threat of substitute are the five competitive forces that drive an industry
A unique value proposition (UV

answersList.content: is a short sentence answering, for whom? Why they need it? How you will meet the need? And finally what you will innovate.

questionsList.content: A value innovation curve (or profile) is a visual portrayal of your product's key features and their performance, relative to alternative products
Low-cost Airline's activity map features:

answersList.content: Low prices, limited passenger service, on-time departures; use of midsize city airports
Point-to-point flights, new aircraft, courteous personnel, motivated employees
Flexible union contracts, fast turnaround, improved food, no luggage checking

questionsList.content: An activity map:

answersList.content: A list of the activities you need to carry out
Shows the key processes and sub-processes that together comprise the system that creates value for your customers
A map showing the steps you need to take to deliver your product

questionsList.content: A product platform is:

answersList.content: A set of shared assets, shared by products
A portfolio of various products and services
A production setup that can produce a variety of products

questionsList.content: The key components of a business plan are: Executive Summary, Company Description, Product/Service Description, Market Opportunity, Sales & Marketing Plan, Management Team, Financial Projections
Mass customization, a special case of platform strategy, is:

answersList.content: Tailoring products to customer preferences provided prior to production
Rapid redesign of mass-produced products to meet new needs

questionsList.content: A "magic circle" feedback loop leads to market leadership because:

answersList.content: Lower costs drive lower prices, higher market share, further declines in cost and so on

questionsList.content: The primary purpose of the 'cap' in a convertible note is:

answersList.content: To establish an arbitrary valuation of an early-stage company
To place a ceiling on the valuation at which the note converts into equity.
To delay the valuation of an early-stage company.
To convert into equity at the value of the next round of financing, regardless of the valuation of that round.

questionsList.content: Which of these represents the accounting equation?
The financial state of having more debt than assets means, you are
Fixed asset accounting

answersList.content: ensures that payroll and employment taxes are kept current.
records if your business keeps surplus cash invested in securities.

questionsList.content: Which statement lists revenues and expenses, and shows the amount of profit a business makes for a specified period of time?
_____ is a measure of how quickly a company can raise money through internal sources by converting assets to cash.
Activities involved in producing and selling goods and services are called _____ activities.
Breakeven point

answersList.content: is the point at which total costs equal gross revenue.
is the point at which fixed costs equal variable costs.
is the point at which total costs equal profit.

questionsList.content: A financial plan for the future based on a single level of operations is called a(n)
What is a Cash flow statement?

answersList.content: A bank statement showing entries in an account
An orderly list showing how much cash went out, and how much came in
A statement showing what the business does with its profits

questionsList.content: A business owned by an individual or small group is referred to as a(n)
Small businesses are

answersList.content: described as being unique and distinctive.

questionsList.content: A prepackaged business bought, rented, or leased from a company is called a(n)
The key market knowledge for a small business

answersList.content: is to know how to seek and obtain external financing.
focuses on its immediate marketplace and communities.
is to know how to cash out through the sale of an initial public offering of stock or through the sale of the company to a larger company.

questionsList.content: Skyler is a songwriter who created a business to sell her songs to the recording industry. Which term best describes Skyler?
Rose runs a landscaping company primarily because she loves gardening. Which term best describes Rose's business?
Entrepreneurs are frequently thought to be individuals who discover market needs and launch new firms to meet those needs
Ken's insurance company continues to increase its profits even during a recent recession that caused several of his competitors to go bankrupt. Which term best describes Ken's company?

answersList.content: Preferred funding source is other people's money
Approach to human resource is to professionalize

questionsList.content: Understanding his weakness in presentation skills, Dylan joined a Toastmasters program. He now successfully presents his business and products to buyers and his sales continually increase. Which entrepreneurial success quality best describes Dylan?

answersList.content: tolerance for risk, ambiguity, and uncertainty

questionsList.content: Before making any decisions, Kyle gathers good information to help navigate through what often seems a complicated web of problems, challenges, and opportunities. Which entrepreneurial success quality best describes Kyle?

answersList.content: tolerance for risk, ambiguity, and uncertainty

questionsList.content: A cap on a convertible note is used to:

answersList.content: Establish the maximum pre-money valuation at which the note will convert
Put a value on the company
Secure the payment of the note upon liquidation
Put a limit on the maximum interest rate payable on the note

questionsList.content: Michelle continually looks for new products and markets to expand her business. Which entrepreneurial success quality best describes Michelle?

answersList.content: tolerance for risk, ambiguity, and uncertainty

questionsList.content: Associates attribute Kayla's business success to her "never give up attitude". Whether it is a problem in operations or selling to a reluctant customer, Kayla continues to work to solve the problem or close the sale. Which entrepreneurial success quality best describes Kayla?

answersList.content: tolerance for risk, ambiguity, and uncertainty

questionsList.content: An individual flees the bureaucratic environment of big firms by going into business for herself. What term best describes this individual?
Richard left a secure job with the government to start a manufacturing company even though he has a young family to take care of. As a successful entrepreneur, what level of risk does Richard accept?
Ken Yip moved to Canada from Trinidad after getting financing for a company start-up with help from a program developed by the Minister of Citizenship. Which term best describes Ken?
Over the past decade Timothy has started seven companies, quickly made them profitable, and then sold them to other entrepreneurs. Which term best describes Timothy?

When Tracee started her business she had three goals in mind: serve her customers well, make a profit, and donate a portion of profits to environmental preservation causes. Which term best describes Tracee?
Patrick, tired of the politics at the company he works for, leaves to start his own accounting firm. Which term best describes Patrick?
In what age category does research show the highest percentage of business start-ups?
Balwant has several ideas for a new business and is learning about becoming an entrepreneur. What will Balwant most likely be concerned about?
Lea sells her handmade products to collectors. Sales could be higher, but she tries to do everything herself and manages people the way she manages her family. Which term best describes Lea?
Surjit, a marketing specialist, and Candice, an investment expert, create a company to sell financial planning services to the public. Which term best describes Surjit and Candice?
Who comprises an entrepreneurial team?

answersList.content: individuals who work together in the same firm as entrepreneurs
the entrepreneur plus the firm's banker, chartered accountant, and lawyer
government officials who provide counseling to aspiring entrepreneurs

questionsList.content: What is an opportunistic entrepreneur expected to be?

answersList.content: unwilling to plan for future growth

questionsList.content: Which of the following describes an opportunistic entrepreneur?

answersList.content: delegates authority in order to grow

questionsList.content: Judy, a mother of five children, developed a fastener to keep sock pairs together in the laundry and sells her invention on the Internet. What is Judy?

Most successful entrepreneurs rarely need to work hard or be persistent. Events and people always tend to just fall in place for them.

Most small businesses have between 150-200 employees.
What are the Revenue Streams that your company or organization will generate?

answersList.content: A revenue stream represents the cash a company generates from each Customer Segment. Revenues minus costs equal to profits.
A revenue stream represents the cost a company generates from each Customer Segment.
A revenue stream represents the cash a company generates from each supplier.

questionsList.content: Three primary rewards of entrepreneurship are thought to be profit, independence, and a satisfying way of life.

answersList.content: Creating a detailed marketing strategy and objectives

What makes a decision "strategic"? Check all that apply.

answersList.content: Results in substantial gains or losses as a result of the decision and its subsequent outcomes
Involves planning actions in an uncertain and unpredictable future

questionsList.content: Strategic decisions typically share which four processes? Choose the four that apply.

answersList.content: Selecting the alternative that best satisfies the evaluation criteria

questionsList.content: The Opportunity Analysis Canvas is designed to help individuals develop which areas? (Check all that apply).

answersList.content: The diversity of the people that you know.
The number of people that you know.

questionsList.content: Why build social capital? (Check all that apply.)

answersList.content: To be a more valuable collaborator
To be a more efficient problem solver

questionsList.content: What four attributes influence an industry's favorability to new firm entry? Choose the four that apply.
What are the four characteristics of industry structure? Choose the four that apply.
Sustainable competitive advantage can only be maintained until competitors are able to duplicate or develop a compelling substitute.
When developing a new product, it is necessary to meet every customer's desire.
Within the product design and development decisions, successful entrepreneurs create multiple products simultaneously that are uniquely positioned for every potential market segment.
It is smart to set your pricing significantly lower than competitors' prices in an effort to capture market share and achieve your revenue goals quickly.
Strategic planning requires near-term commitment for long-term benefits.
What factors may make it economically unattractive to produce your anticipated product or service? Choose all that apply.

answersList.content: Over-designing the product to the point where the price is beyond your customer
Designing the product for a market niche that is too small to generate the desired revenue goals of your company
Agreeing to minimum production volume requirements that exceed your ability to pay for the production costs and invest in marketing appropriately

questionsList.content: What are the four key items do customers want to know? (Choose Four)

answersList.content: How does this impact my end state?
Do I have chemistry with this seller?
What are the technical specifications of the solution?

questionsList.content: Which "customers" do most solutions involve? (Choose All That Apply)
A "customer archetype" is a "customer type"?

Anti-dilution provisions in financing documents protect:

answersList.content: (a) founders, by preventing their ownership in a company from being significantly reduced by new investors
(b) investors from dilution in the event of stock splits, stock dividends or sales of stock at a price lower than that the investor paid
(c) employees who have options issued from the company's option pool

questionsList.content: Bridge financing is a term that means:

answersList.content: An interim financing round that provides cash to a company that is anticipating either a larger financing round, a merger or an initial public offering
A special kind of construction loan
An interim financing round that provides cash to a company that "bridges" the gap between cash on hand and the next monthly payroll

questionsList.content: The key components of a business plan are:

answersList.content: Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow Statement
Company Description, Product Description, Market Analysis, Headcount Plan, Customer List, Patents
Executive Summary, Company Description, Product/Service Description, Market Opportunity, Sales & Marketing Plan, Management Team, Financial Projections
Early-stage companies do not need to write business plans because they are new to the market and too many variables are unknown

questionsList.content: The 4 "M's" that investors look for in a company are:

answersList.content: Momentum, Management Team, Market Potential, Money
Monthly Sales, Management Team, Money, Mystery
Momentum, Monthly Sales, Money, Management Team

questionsList.content: Which of the following is false about a Term Sheet?

answersList.content: It includes the pre-money valuation of the company.
It is binding on the investor for 60 days.
It specifies the use of proceeds.
It specifies the form of investment.

questionsList.content: Series A, Series B and Series C preferred refers to the:

answersList.content: Priority of the preferred stock in liquidation.
Nothing - they are simply given a letter to distinguish each preferred stock issuance.

questionsList.content: An investor invests $2.5 million on $5 million post-money valuation in a participating Preferred Stock. The company subsequently sells for $10 million. What is the ownership of the Preferred investor and how much of the $10 million will the investor receive?
Which of the following statements about a balance sheet is correct?
What is the simplest form of corporate structure?
Early stage companies, owing to the lack of history and collateral, rarely raise money via debt with one notable exception, which is:

answersList.content: Preferred stock, with a dividend that acts like an interest rate
Convertible notes, which are a hybrid, more closely related to equity

questionsList.content: Which of the following is TRUE regarding intermediary sources of capital? (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: They can introduce you to other financing sources.
They are a prime source of early-stage capital.
They may charge a fee for their services.

questionsList.content: Strategic or corporate venture capital firms make investments:

answersList.content: Primarily outside of their core businesses to diversify their risk
Within their core businesses to achieve financial and strategic returns
To build risk portfolios with excess cash of the corporation

questionsList.content: The primary sources of seed and early stage capital include the following: (Select all that apply.)
Which of the following statements is CORRECT about friends and family investors? (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Terms should always be put into writing
They invest because of the relationship to the founder(s)
When discussing an investment with a friends and family investor, the entrepreneur should clearly discuss risk versus reward
Only equity investments are allowed given the relationship to the founder

questionsList.content: According to the U.

answersList.content: Federal Reserve, what percentage of all startups are bootstrapped?

questionsList.content: What is the primary benefit to the entrepreneur of bootstrapping his/her company:

answersList.content: The longer time it takes to get to the market allows the entrepreneur time to make sure the product works perfectly
If successful, the entrepreneur retains a greater share of the exit value (or a higher valuation during fundraising)
Bartering with established companies develops lasting relationships that would not otherwise be developed
It allows the entrepreneur to be creative in finding ways to achieve progress without money

questionsList.content: One of the disadvantages of an entrepreneur going into an accelerator program is that the entrepreneur:

answersList.content: Spends a lot of time meeting investors interested in future financing rounds
Is often provided new office space
Must give up equity to participate
Spends too much time receiving training that strengthens his/her business expertise

questionsList.content: Which of the following is true about angel investors:

answersList.content: They invest twice as much as venture capital funds do each year
They provide critical seed capital that other funding sources do not focus on
They are often motivated by factors other than financial return

questionsList.content: Seed or early-stage investors typically invest in companies that have all of the following attributes: (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Is seeking funding of $5 million or less

questionsList.content: Which of the following statements is correct about the due diligence process? (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Due diligence is not time-consuming if done properly
Due diligence is a two-way process
Prospective investors will want to investigate legal, financial and strategic matters
The key to success in due diligence is advance preparation and cooperation among the parties

questionsList.content: Which of the following items discovered in the due diligence process could be a cause for concern by an investor, which could ultimately lead to a decision by the investor to NOT invest?

answersList.content: Large accounts payable for goods and services that are all "current" (i.e., within the terms offered by the vendor)
A large shareholder loan that will be paid upon completion of the current financing
Significantly related party transactions that are properly documented and performed on an arms-length basis
Accounts receivable from 20 very small customers which are 10 days past due

questionsList.content: When negotiating terms sheets with prospective investors, which of the following are correct? (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Experience from past deals should be used to negotiate the current term sheet.
Don't take everything the other side says at face value - they may be posturing.
Both the investor and entrepreneur need to win.
Compromise indicates to the investor that the entrepreneur is desperate for funding.

questionsList.content: Valuation of startup companies can be done objectively by following traditional valuation methods.
Choose the statement that is NOT correct.

answersList.content: If the average PE ratio of companies similar to your company in the industry in which you will operate is 15X, and your projected after tax earnings in year 5 is $3 million, a reasonable exit valuation for your company in year 5 is $45 million
The Wiltbank Study indicates that investors expect an IRR of 15% in six years

questionsList.content: What factor(s) can impact the valuation of your company?
Equity securities include all of the following: (Select all that apply.)
Which of the following statements about term sheets is correct? (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: They state the pre-money valuation and the amount of the investment.
They are binding on the investor for 60 days.
No shop clauses are beneficial to the investor but not the company.
They normally include a statement on the use of the proceeds of the investment.

questionsList.content: A cap on a convertible note is used to:

answersList.content: Establish the maximum pre-money valuation at which the note will convert
Put a limit on the maximum interest rate payable on the note
Secure the payment of the note upon liquidation
Put a value on the company

questionsList.content: Which of the following is not a characteristic of Preferred Stock?

answersList.content: Preferred stock is an equity instrument with some characteristic of debt

Preferred stock shareholders receive the greater of the original purchase plus accrued dividends OR a percentage of the liquidation proceeds on an 'as-converted" basis in a standard liquidation of the company
Anti-dilution provisions protect the preferred shareholder's pro-rata interests
It is the financing vehicle of choice for venture capitalists

questionsList.content: Which of the following statements is correct about what prospective investors want to see when you pitch your company to them? (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Investors don't care how or if your product works because they assume it does or will
Why customers will buy your product/solution
You and your team have an in-depth knowledge of the market

questionsList.content: When an entrepreneur tells an investor "I have no competition", what the investor hears and thinks is:

answersList.content: (a) The market the entrepreneur is going after must not be very big if no other competitors are entering it
(b) That's great! This entrepreneur is pretty smart to have built a product in a big market that has no competing products to worry about
(c) This entrepreneur doesn't know the market very well

questionsList.content: What is Guy Kawasaki's 10/20/30 rule?
What is the one attribute of every "perfect" pitch deck, regardless of its size?

answersList.content: They have an appendix with additional slides not covered during the actual pitch session.
They all have at least one embedded video.
They have bright colors, professional fonts and creative graphics to enhance the presentation.

questionsList.content: When discussing/presenting the Competition slide in a pitch deck, which of the following statements is correct?

answersList.content: Point out why your product or solution is better but never "knock" the competition.
Highlight how bad the competitors' product is.
If there is no competition in your market, this slide is unnecessary.
Never discuss competitive products by name - otherwise, your prospective investors may seek out your competitors and invest in their products.

questionsList.content: The appendix is useful for:

answersList.content: Putting information into the deck that you want to have available in the event questions arise after the pitch to which these slides relate.
Hiding problematic information that you'd rather not talk about.
Providing a list of all slides in the deck.
Nothing - an appendix should never be included in your pitch deck.

questionsList.content: When you "tell your story" to prospectors, you should do all of the following: (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Be prepared to go into great details, especially if your business is complicated.
Discuss who you are, where you're from and why you're starting the company.
Be genuine, passionate and try to connect directly with the audience.
Tell interesting stories constructed from anecdotes from your life that an investor might think is interesting.

questionsList.content: Which of the following is a "Don't" that should be avoided in making an investor pitch?
Which of the following are "Do's" of making a pitch: (Select all that apply.)

answersList.content: Give a product demo, if applicable.
Forecast 'hockey stick' growth to ensure the investor sees that market as having significant potential.
Highlight your and your management team's prior successes.

questionsList.content: Assuming that an entrepreneur must speak to between 10 to 15 prospective investors to actually have one investor "close"; that the amount of each investor's investment is $50,000; and you are trying to raise a minimum of $1 million. How many prospective investors must the entrepreneur speak to before they successfully raise $1 million?

answersList.content: It is impossible to estimate based on the data provided.
